preview_text:
The model favors Germany at 84%, with Curaçao's win probability below 1%. The underdog has no realistic path to victory in the statistical forecast, though the model assigns Curaçao a non-zero chance reflective of match uncertainty inherent in any fixture.

The 15% draw probability suggests the model sees a plausible route to a stalemate, though a German win remains the clear modal outcome. The win gap of roughly 83 percentage points indicates a heavily asymmetric contest; Curaçao's chances concentrate almost entirely on avoiding defeat rather than winning outright.

The sportsbook consensus diverges meaningfully from the model, rating Germany at 91% and collapsing the draw to 6%. The market is substantially more confident in a German victory and far less confident in a draw—a gap of 9 percentage points on the win side and 9 points on the draw. Polymarket prices align more closely with the sportsbook view. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these assessments.
